name: shepherds-shelter
summary: A stone-and-turf shelter on a hillside — half-buried in the slope, low doorway, a single room with a fire-ring. A shepherd's refuge from weather and wolves.

primary_palette.note: central fire-ring, bedroll on stone, log for a seat
doubles as a lambing pen in storm weather — hay, trough for a sickly lamb
minimal refuge — fire, bedroll, a pack, nothing else

story_prompts: shelter is empty but stocked — shepherd left firewood and bread 'for whoever needs it'
shepherd inside, wary of strangers — shares fire, watches his crook
wolves took the flock last night — the shepherd is mourning, or hunting revenge
the dog is dead at the door, the shepherd is missing, the fire is cold
a hiding place — not a shepherd at all, a fugitive wearing the part

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: dim — the shelter is roofed, small windows or none at all, firelight dominates

anti_patterns: it's small — do not over-prop, a shepherd's shelter holds one person and their gear
half-buried in a slope is the point — at least one wall should read as 'earth/stone', not built
no livestock living inside — a sickly lamb at most, the flock is outdoors
the fire is central, not against a wall — vented through a roof hole
